characters.name: Beatriz Aurora "Betty" Pinzón Solano(De Mendoza)
characters.description: Beautiful All Along: Betty's not really ugly, but she is unkempt because of her braces, glasses that obscure her face, and unfavorable dresses. Her poor choice of clothes came from the belief that no good dress would look good on her, and the series makes sure that you know this. As soon as she makes the most base-level changes to her appearance (cutting her front bangs and getting smaller glasses), she looks fantastic. She even keeps her braces post-makeover! Because You Were Nice to Me: She commits acts she never would otherwise because of her moral compass, all because Armando backed her up when hardly anyone else would...despite his outbursts. Braces-Based Bullying: Part of the reason Betty is relentlessly picked on by others is because of her braces. Braces of Orthodontic Overkill: Part of her "ugliness" stems from her obvious braces. Interestingly, she keeps them post-makeover. Broken Tears: Upon discoveringArmando's deception by pretending to love herfrom Mario's letter for him, she immediately bursts into heavy tears so much that she nearly faints and needs Catalina's help to move.

characters.name: Julia Solano De Pinzón
characters.description: Mama Bear: Despite she is less paranoid than her husband about Betty's social life, she is not afraid to scold Armando —her daughter's boss who is a rich and powerful man— upon learning his wrongdoings toward her daughter.

characters.name: Hérmes Pinzón Galarza
characters.description: Amazingly Embarrassing Parents: Overprotective, and extremely proud of his daughter, Betty has to deal with constant embarrassment because of this two traits. Papa Wolf: Despite he seems overprotective toward his daughter, he doesn't hesitate to defend and protect her from anyone who potentially hurts her regardless of their status. Like he consoled her when she went through a painful breakup with her first boyfriend, Miguel, and he also dares to engage in a physical fight with Roman who often teases and harasses her.

characters.name: Nicolás Mora
characters.description: Like a Son to Me: He is like a son to Betty's parents and like a brother to Betty. Love Martyr: Despite he knows Patricialoves him only for his moneyand treats him like a servant, he still willingly tolerates her. The Rival:Played for Laughs. Armando briefly considered him one upon noticing hisextremelyclose friendship with Betty as he assumed to have made Betty to grow distant from himnoteUnaware that it's all part ofBetty's game plan to toy Armandoas a retaliation for his deception. Strong Family Resemblance: He and his mother share the exact similar looks; same big glasses and same hairstyle.

characters.name: Armando Mendoza Sáenz
characters.description: All Girls Want Bad Boys: He is a notoriouswomanizerandshort-tempered, but Marcela and Betty are crazy in love with him. Although Betty is aware ofMichell's feelings for her, but she still pines for Armando. The Atoner: He tries fixing all his mistakes with varying degrees of success.Well, ultimately success. Badass in a Nice Suit: He always wear suits but he is quite excellent in street fighting, such as; when Roman and his gang interrupt his and Betty's moment, he defends Betty by successfully beating them before Don Hérmes interrupts and assists Armando, finally chasing the gang away. Becoming the Mask: He only datesBettyto help save his company and arguably due to a repressed jealousy for her perceived boyfriend (but only out of a possessive liking towards her). He winds up loving her more than he's loved any other woman in his life and it drives him for the rest of the story. Berserk Button: It doesn’t take long for his button to becomeanyonewho says something bad about Beatriz. If you insult heryou better hope you’re his best friend and can get away with it most of the time, because you’re messing with him personally. Most of his violent acts in the series have been directly related to protecting Beatriz, or at least, him thinking that he is.

characters.name: Marcela Valencia
characters.description: Good Is Not Nice: She often belittles Betty due to the latter's poor sense of fashion and style, but she never tries to harm or confront the latter for technically "stealing" her fiancé. I Want My Beloved to Be Happy: She loves Armando so much that she takes his well-being as her serious concern, even after they break up for good. Upon learning that Ecomoda would be ruined if Betty resignsnotedue to her eventual resentment toward Armandoand Armando —who thinks no one (including himself) could keep Ecomoda's stability as good as Betty— not only decides to quit Ecomoda, but even goes as far as to exile himself somewhere unknown in hopingBetty would change her mind to stay working in Ecomoda, it finally leaves her no choice but to encourage Betty to forgive and take Armando back since she knows how much Armando loves Betty and still pines for the latter. Love Martyr: Despite she is aware of her fiancé's constant cheating, she always takes him back. Even when he finally breaks up with her for good, she still begs him to stay with her. Please, Don't Leave Me: Says this to Armando after he officially decides to break up with her. Red Oni, Blue Oni: She is the blue to Patricia's red.

characters.name: Mario Calderón
characters.description: The Casanova: Calderón is known for this. He’s an expert at figuring out what women want and their behavior to a sickening level. Break Them by Talking: Tries to pull this with Armando, and is partly successful with his parting words. Lack of Empathy: While he certainly doesn’t lack charisma, Calderón’s motivations are always calculated and cold, and he doesn’t even show any level of empathy for the woman he hurtas he reads her diary. Manipulative Bastard: He's notably good at getting Armando to distrust the woman he outright says he trusts the most in the whole world, manipulating his obsession with protecting his family’s patrimony and his unrealized jealousy to get him to agree to his plans to keep Ecomoda. Red Oni, Blue Oni: The red to Armando's blue.

characters.name: Patricia Fernández
characters.description: Hair Flip: She famously often does this to emphasize her vain and narcissistic personality. Gold Digger: The fact that she left college just to marry a very wealthy man cannot just be a coincidence. Also, she tried to manipulate two men,Daniel and Mario, with afake pregnancyjust so she could get away with as much money as possible before being discovered (although she ultimately failed); later she attempts to steal Nicolas away from Betty while she's under the impression that he's her rich boyfriend, going as far as sleeping with him in two occasions despite the fact that she makes no secret that she's repulsed by him. Princess in Rags: Lack of money doesn't stop her to keep trying to maintain the image she used to have. Including the haughty attitude, towards those she still consider 'below her', like her co-workers. Red Oni, Blue Oni: The red to Marcela's blue. Rich Bitch: What she used to be, before losing the 'rich' title.

characters.name: Hugo Lombardi
characters.description: Always Camp: Hugo is a fashion designer. Camp Gay: Hugo is an effeminate, melodramatic, extravagant man who loves fashion. Effeminate Voice: And how...? Odd Friendship: He loves Ines, despite the fact she is around three decades older than him, a very prim and proper lady and friends with the group of women he constantly berates. When she suddenly faints in his arms, he is distraught and all but demands she stay in his home so he could look after her.

characters.name: Saúl Gutiérrez
characters.description: Gratuitous English: He often peppers his speeches with English.

characters.name: Aura María
characters.description: Good Bad Girl: She is often seenhooking up with some menbefore eventually settles with Freddy, but she's loyal toward her gang and Betty. Master of the Mixed Message: Can't seem to agree on what kind of behavior she wants to see from Freddy - She pushes him aside on several occasions only to get mad when he flirts with other women, and most egregiously: She spends the night with him then says he's just one of her "friends". Ms. Fanservice: She is actually very pretty and often wears skimpy dresses that show off her skin, especially herlegsandheaving buxom.

characters.name: Sandra Patiño
characters.description: Hair-Trigger Temper: She tends to become violent wherever she feels offense towards herself or her friends. Huge Schoolgirl: To put it simply, she is an adult-in-the-workforce extension of the concept. She is very sensitive over being way taller than the average Colombian female, and even taller than the average Colombianmale. Any mention of her height in a negative context, and she simply snaps into physical violence. It also wrecked her romantic prospects: since average men are intimidated by her height (and, frankly, over her prickly persona too), she is only interested on men taller than her; but since she hasn't found one yet, it makes her dread to become anOld Maid, only reinforcing her insecurities. Statuesque Stunner: She is the tallest among the gang and even slightly towers Armando and Mario.

characters.name: Bertha De Gonzáles
characters.description: Big Beautiful Woman: Despite she's described as fat, but she's actually more of curvy and is in fact quite attractive. Big Eater: She's often seen sneaking to eat a snack, even in the middle of working hour whenever Armando isn't around.

characters.name: Inés "Incesita" Ramírez
characters.description: Morality Pet: To Hugo. Hugo never holds his tongue when it comes to insulting "The Ugly Squads" members, but she is his only exception. Hugo cares so much for her that he often accompanies and takes care of her when she got sick to the point of sleeping on the same bed with her like a mother and son.
